text:
Admission Date:  1990-04-16       Discharge Date:  2011-09-22

Date of Birth:   1973-09-14       Sex:  M

Service:  MICU ORANG

CHIEF COMPLAINT:  Drug overdose.

HISTORY OF THE PRESENT ILLNESS:  Mr. Potter is a
41-year-old male with a history of bipolar disorder, who
decided on the night of admission to make an attempt on his
life.  In this attempt, he ingested an unknown quantity of
both valproic acid and lithium tablets, as well as drinking
alcohol.  The patient was brought to the Upland Hills Hlth Emergency Room after being found
with evidence of intoxication including vomiting, stumbling,
encopresis, and unresponsiveness to both peers and staff at
his home.  He reportedly fell several times while he
attempted to walk.  He was subsequently brought to the
emergency room and then admitted to the Intensive Care Unit
Service.  The patient noted some blurred vision, mild
abdominal pain, which had improved upon arrival in the
emergency department.  He denied any shortness of breath,
chest pain, or headache.

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:
1.  Hypertension.
2.  Bipolar disorder.

ALLERGIES:  No known drug allergies.

SOCIAL HISTORY:  The patient drinks a six pack of beer per
day.  He smokes one pack of cigarettes per day.  He has lived
in group home since moving from [**State 760**] following
separation from his wife four months ago.  Currently, he
lives in a group home.  He denies any intravenous drug abuse.

MEDICATIONS ON ADMISSION:
1.  Cozaar 100 mg PO q.h.s.
2.  Lithium 1500 mg PO q.h.s.
3.  Risperdal 5 mg PO q.h.s.
4.  Lamictal 75 mg PO q.d.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:  Examination revealed the following:
Vital signs:  Afebrile.  Blood pressure 130/90, pulse 110,
respiratory rate 16.  SPO2 97% on room air.  HEENT:
Normocephalic, atraumatic.  Extraocular muscles are intact.
Pupils are equal, reactive, and round.  Sclerae were
anicteric.  Oropharynx was dry.  Neck was supple without
lymphadenopathy.  Lungs were clear to auscultation
bilaterally.  Normal S1, normal S2, tachycardia.  ABDOMEN:
Positive bowel sounds, soft, mild tenderness in the
periumbilical region.  EXTREMITIES:  No clubbing, cyanosis or
edema.  NEUROLOGICAL:  Alert and oriented times three,
positive tremor.  Deep tendon reflexes 0/4 in upper
extremities and lower extremities bilaterally.

LABORATORY DATA:  Labs on admission revealed the following:
Sodium 14l, potassium 4.7, chloride 103, CO2 22, BUN 10,
creatinine 1.3, glucose 93, white blood cell count 16.2.
Hemoglobin 13, hematocrit 38, platelet count 659.  Valproic
acid level 513 mg per milliliter.  Lithium level 3.8 mEq per
liter.  Urine toxicology screen negative.  Serum toxicology
screen negative.  Ethanol level 94 mg per deciliter.
Urinalysis:  0 red blood cells, 5 white blood cells.
Specific gravity 1.01.  ALT 14, AST 21.  Alkaline phosphatase
40.  Amylase 112.  Total bilirubin 0.1.  Ammonia 220.  ABG
7.46/37/67 on room air.

EKG:  Sinus tachycardia.  QTC 0.41, QRS 110.  Poor R-wave
progression.

HOSPITAL COURSE:

#1.  RESPIRATORY:  Mr. Potter has remained stable from a
respiratory standpoint.  On initial chest x-ray he was felt
to have a right middle lobe infiltrate, possibly associated
with aspiration.  Later chest x-ray demonstrated bilateral
lower lobe atelectasis.  He was started on Levaquin and
Flagyl for aspiration pneumonia.  During the course of
several nights while in the hospital, Mr. Mack oxygen
saturation has dropped to the mid 90s.  This was felt to be
related to sleep apnea and we recommend a workup for sleep
apnea as an outpatient.

#2.  CARDIOVASCULAR:  Mr. Potter has remained
hemodynamically stable throughout the course of the
hospitalization.  The blood pressure has been mildly elevated
and he was restarted on his home medication of Cozaar at
100  mg q.h.s.

#3.  INFECTIOUS DISEASE:  Given the likelihood for
aspiration, Mr. Potter was started on Flagyl and Levaquin
for a 10-day course of PO antibiotics.

#4.  TOXICOLOGY:  The toxicology service was asked to consult
on Mr. Potter from the emergency department.  He had a
whole bowel irrigation with GoLYTELY solution.  He was also
treated with three doses of activated charcoal, which was
used to enhance valproic acid clearance.  For the first
twenty-four hour of this hospitalization, he received normal
saline at 250 cc per hour.  He was also started on
Levocarnitine 50 mg per kilogram per day for carnitine
deficiency, decreased ammonia levels and improved lethargy
associated with VPA toxicity.  He was also started on
multivitamin and thiamine.

Over the course of the hospitalization, the drug levels have
steadily declined.  Lithium level started at 3.8, moved to
2.4, then 1.7, 1.3, then 1.2 and now 1.0.  Depakote level
started at 368, progressively declined to 227, then 106, then
76, then 49 and then 33.  With supportive care, it is felt
that the acute toxicological issues have now resolved.

#5.  RENAL:  The renal team was consulted with the potential
for urgent hemodialysis give the large congestion of toxins.
The renal team felt that he was not in acute need for
hemodialysis given his creatinine.  Initial creatinine was
1.3 and that has improved to 1.0.

#6.  HEMATOLOGY:  Hematocrit has remained stable.  On the day
of dictation, the hematocrit is 35.7.  Platelets have also
remained stable.

#7.  FLUIDS, ELECTROLYTES, AND NUTRITION:  During the course
of the hospitalization, Mr. Potter has been restarted on
PO fluids and food.  He has done well.  He notes that his
appetite is down and he has been drinking increasing amounts
of fluids.  Upon discharge, we are currently restricting him
to two liters of PO fluids per day.

#8.  PSYCHIATRY:  The psychiatric team has been asked to
consult on Mr. Potter.  They have seen him and feel that
he is in need of inpatient psychiatric care.  He has been
placed on 1:1 observations.  We have restarted the Risperdal
at 1 mg q.h.s.  The psychiatric team will continue to follow
him and facilitate placement in a psychiatric facility.

#9. CONDITION ON DISCHARGE:  Stable.

DISCHARGE STATUS:  Inpatient psychiatric facility.

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:  Polysubstance overdose.














DISCHARGE MEDICATIONS:
1.  Risperdal 1 mg PO q.h.s.
2.  Folic acid 1 mg PO q.d.
3.  Thiamine HCL 100 mg PO q.d.
4.  Multivitamins one capsule PO q.d.
5.  Protonix 40 mg PO q.d.
6.  Losartan potassium (Cozaar) 100 mg PO q.h.s.
7.  Levofloxacin 250 mg PO q.24h. times ten days, started on
05-08 to finish on 1993-06-13.
8.  Metronidazole 500 mg PO t.i.d. for ten days started on
1990-04-16 to finish on 1993-06-13.




                     DR.Julie,Daniel 12-981

Dictated By:Evans
